title: Cathode UI manifest
description: Machine-readable registry of every Cathode primitive. Consumed by AI coding agents + the @cathode-ui/mcp server. Regenerate via `npm run gen:manifest` after component changes.

adapters.description: Framework adapters this manifest ships — matches the keys under each component's `adapters` field. 'react' is the default; future phases add 'vue', 'svelte', 'solid', 'compose', etc.

imports.description: Copy-pastable import lines for common setup tasks.

tokens.description: Reference to the full token source of truth.

name.description: PascalCase component name as exported from @cathode-ui/react.

import.description: Single-line copy-pastable import statement. Backwards-compatibility alias for `adapters.react.import` — older MCP clients read this flat field. New clients should prefer `adapters[framework].import`.

adapters.description: Framework-specific import + examples. Each key is a framework adapter ('react', 'vue', 'svelte', 'solid', 'compose', …). The top-level `import` and `examples` fields mirror `adapters.react` for backwards compat; new adapters should only be looked up through this object.

summary.description: One-paragraph description of what the component is.

whenToUse.description: One imperative sentence — when should an author reach for this component?

motionStates.description: Named animation states the component drives (e.g. 'idle', 'press', 'enter', 'exit').

type.description: Prose type description — not strict TypeScript.

default.description: Default value when omitted. May be undefined, string, number, boolean, array, or JSON-stringified.

component.description: The confusable sibling component name, or a descriptive phrase (e.g. 'Button variant=danger', 'inline <code>').

picker.description: One-sentence rule for choosing between this component and the named sibling.

import.description: Framework-specific single-line import statement, e.g. `import { Button } from '@cathode-ui/vue';`.

examples.description: Framework-idiomatic usage snippets — JSX for React, SFC-style for Vue, component markup for Svelte, etc.
